title: Join FastF1 Exogenous Race-State Features
summary: FastF1 exposes weather, track status, session status, race-control messages, telemetry, and position data; those are exactly the exogenous triggers that often cause non-tyre-driven pit calls.
rationale: FastF1 exposes weather, track status, session status, race-control messages, telemetry, and position data; those are exactly the exogenous triggers that often cause non-tyre-driven pit calls. This is a strong adjacent signal because the provided table is lap-level but strategy changes are often caused by VSC/SC, rain onset, or traffic windows rather than pure wear.
implementation_hint: Create a side dataset keyed by Year/Race/LapNumber from FastF1 with track_status, rainfall, track temp, wind, total_laps, race-control flags, and simple traffic metrics such as distance/gap to driver ahead when available; test a narrow ablation first: safety-car flag, rainfall flag, session total_laps, and wet/dry transition indicators.
expected_effect: Most likely gain is better recall on atypical stops and wet/intermediate races, plus improved generalization across tracks and seasons.
risk: Entity matching across race names/years can be messy, and telemetry-derived gap features can accumulate integration error if computed over long windows.
